fixhints.generatedCode:
from jinja2 import Template


def render_user_profile_bio(bio_content: str) -> str:
    """
    Render user's profile bio content into HTML format.

    Args:
        bio_content: User input for their personal bio description

    Returns:
        Rendered HTML string containing the formatted bio
    """
    # Use a static, trusted template to eliminate SSTI risk (user input is only passed as data)
    # Explicit escape filter sanitizes HTML/JavaScript in user input to prevent XSS,
    # independent of environment autoescape settings
    template = Template("{{ bio_content|escape }}")
    return template.render(bio_content=bio_content)

from jinja2 import Template


def render_user_profile_bio(bio_content: str) -> str:
    """
    Render user's profile bio content into HTML format.

    Args:
        bio_content: User input for their personal bio description

    Returns:
        Rendered HTML string containing the formatted bio
    """
    # Use a static, application-defined template to prevent SSTI (user input is never parsed as template code)
    # Enable autoescaping to automatically escape HTML in user input and prevent XSS
    template = Template("{{ bio_content }}", autoescape=True)
    return template.render(bio_content=bio_content)

from jinja2 import Environment from markupsafe import escape, Markup # Custom linebreaksbr filter to replicate expected functionality (not built into Jinja2) def linebreaksbr(value: str) -> Markup: """Convert newlines to
tags with XSS protection. Normalizes all line ending formats, escapes input, then adds
tags. Returns safe HTML to ensure
tags are rendered correctly. """ # Normalize Windows (\r\n) and old Mac (\r) line endings to standard \n normalized = value.replace('\r\n', '\n').replace('\r', '\n') # Escape user input to prevent XSS attacks escaped = escape(normalized) # Replace newlines with
and mark as safe HTML return Markup(escaped.replace('\n', '
')) # Create Jinja2 environment with autoescaping enabled for security env = Environment(autoescape=True) # Register custom filter with the environment env.filters['linebreaksbr'] = linebreaksbr # Predefined Jinja2 template for user bio section # Autoescaping mitigates XSS attacks by escaping user-provided HTML/JavaScript # The linebreaksbr filter converts newlines to
tags for proper HTML formatting BIO_TEMPLATE = env.from_string( """
{{ bio_content|linebreaksbr }}
""" ) def render_user_profile_bio(bio_content: str) -> str: """Render a user's self-provided bio into secure, formatted HTML. fixhints.generatedCode:
from jinja2 import Template


def render_user_profile_bio(bio_content: str) -> str:
    """
    Render user's profile bio content into HTML format.

    Args:
        bio_content: User input for their personal bio description

    Returns:
        Rendered HTML string containing the formatted bio
    """
    # Use a static, trusted template with autoescaping enabled
    # Prevents SSTI by treating user input as a variable instead of template code
    # Autoescaping sanitizes HTML/JS to prevent XSS attacks
    template = Template("{{ bio }}", autoescape=True)
    return template.render(bio=bio_content)
fixhints.functionalReason:
